page: Microsoft Azure DC16es_v5 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the DCEV5 server family. Powered by an Intel processor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance features 16 dedicated virtual CPUs (vCPUs) and 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is designated as Premium Storage capable, allowing users to attach external high-performance storage volumes.

Without local storage, the DC16es_v5 relies entirely on network-attached storage, which represents a tradeoff for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space. However, the dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities without resource contention. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the instance provides a balanced resource configuration suitable for enterprise applications that require dedicated compute resources and premium external storage integration without the overhead of local disk maintenance. This configuration is suitable for general-purpose workloads, relational databases, and application servers that utilize external storage.
description: The Microsoft Azure DC16es_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the DCEV5 family, configured with 16 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 64.0 GB of system memory. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 4.0 GB per core. It features no local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance external storage attachments. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures predictable performance for workloads that do not require local disk space. This server is suitable for general-purpose enterprise applications, database hosting, and application servers requiring consistent compute resources and external storage configurations.
